_meta._note: BIA-specific structure ported from langgraph-python. The D6 probe (d5-tool-rendering-custom-catchall.ts) types two direct prompts — 'Forecast Tokyo through the wildcard renderer' (get_weather) and 'Quote AAPL through the wildcard renderer' (get_stock_price) — and asserts BOTH render through [data-testid=custom-wildcard-card] AND that the narration bubble contains the canonical phrase 'rendered through the custom wildcard catchall'. BIA runs these tools server-side via TanStack chat() and reprompts the LLM with the tool result, and its /v1/responses endpoint rewrites tool_call_ids to runtime `fc-…` values, so the LGP toolCallId-keyed narration never matches. Each pill is therefore a (sequenceIndex:0 emitter, narration fallback) pair per PARITY_NOTES: the emitter fires on the FIRST request for the pill prompt (counter starts at 0) and emits the tool call; the BIA reprompt iteration falls through the now-exhausted emitter to the userMessage-only narration fallback (no tool call), so the loop converges and the canonical narration lands. sequenceIndex (not hasToolResult:false) preserves multi-turn correctness — hasToolResult is computed across the whole thread. The four real UI pills (SF weather, flights, d20, chain) from suggestions.ts are also ported for the e2e spec; they use disjoint userMessages so they cannot cross-route with the two probe prompts.

fixtures._comment: PROBE pill 1 emit — 'Forecast Tokyo through the wildcard renderer'. sequenceIndex:0 gates the FIRST request so it emits get_weather; the BIA server-tool reprompt falls through to the narration fallback below.

match.userMessage: Forecast Tokyo through the wildcard renderer

fixtures._comment: PROBE pill 1 narration fallback — fires on the BIA reprompt iteration (emitter above exhausted). Emits the canonical custom-catchall phrase the probe asserts. No tool call, so the reprompt loop converges.

match.userMessage: Forecast Tokyo through the wildcard renderer

response.content: Tokyo is 22°C and partly cloudy — the custom wildcard renderer should show the tool card above — rendered through the custom wildcard catchall.

fixtures._comment: PROBE pill 2 emit — 'Quote AAPL through the wildcard renderer'. sequenceIndex:0 gates the FIRST request to emit get_stock_price (a DIFFERENT tool through the same wildcard renderer); the reprompt falls through to the narration fallback below.

match.userMessage: Quote AAPL through the wildcard renderer

fixtures._comment: PROBE pill 2 narration fallback — fires on the BIA reprompt iteration. Emits the canonical custom-catchall phrase the probe asserts (turn-2 bubble text check).

match.userMessage: Quote AAPL through the wildcard renderer

response.content: AAPL is trading at $338.37, down 2.96% — rendered through the custom wildcard catchall renderer.

fixtures._comment: UI pill 'Weather in SF' emit — 'What's the weather in San Francisco?'. sequenceIndex:0 emitter; reprompt falls through to narration below.

match.userMessage: What's the weather in San Francisco?

fixtures._comment: UI pill 'Weather in SF' narration fallback.

match.userMessage: What's the weather in San Francisco?

response.content: San Francisco is currently 68°F and sunny with light winds — rendered through the custom wildcard catchall.

fixtures._comment: UI pill 'Find flights' emit — 'Find flights from SFO to JFK.'. sequenceIndex:0 emitter; reprompt falls through to narration below.

match.userMessage: Find flights from SFO to JFK.
Find flights from SFO to JFK.

response.content: Three flights from SFO to JFK — United UA231 at 08:15 ($348), Delta DL412 at 11:20 ($312), and JetBlue B6722 at 17:05 ($289).

fixtures._comment: UI pill 'Roll a d20' — five sequential roll_d20 emissions [7,14,3,19,20] gated by sequenceIndex (BIA server-tool reprompt increments the per-pill counter each iteration). The 5th roll is 20; the narration fallback fires once the sequence is exhausted.
UI pill 'Roll a d20' narration fallback — fires once the five sequenceIndex-gated rolls are exhausted.

response.content: Rolled the d20 five times — landed on 20 on the final roll.

fixtures._comment: UI pill 'Chain tools' emit — three tool calls in one assistant turn (get_weather Tokyo + search_flights SFO->Tokyo + roll_d20=11). sequenceIndex:0 emitter; reprompt falls through to narration below.

match.userMessage: Chain a few tools in this single turn
Chain a few tools in this single turn

response.content: Done — Tokyo is sunny, three flights found, and the d20 came up 11.
